change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 93 → 1079 chars (91.4% increase) since c2 review on 2026-04-07. Post-hoc/timestamp test boilerplate was added but never adversarially reviewed. Two c2 moderate findings (duplicate sentence, missing WIN-040-specific engagement) remain unaddressed after 15 days.
our_argument_summary: WIN-040 is reviewed in a grouped block spanning 9 WINs (040-043, 057b, 059-061, 063) that all draw from INTERMAGNET/NOAA/Tomsk datasets already exploited by earlier WINs. The grouped detail_evidence argues these are data-reuse duplicates rather than independent confirmations. A generic post-hoc/timestamp test paragraph was appended to detail_verdict_text. Verdict: Std Model Explains — non-discriminating; dome observes position from same data as WMM with no unique prediction derived from dome geometry.

relevant_files: raw-text/03-predictions.txt (W022: 'SAA western cell position: West of 45 deg W, cells separated (PENDING)', registered 2026-03-12)
monitor.py not present in raw-text archive — no domain for SAA cell longitude identified in any prior cycle
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automated monitoring pipeline for WIN-040. W022 is a structural/geographic threshold: 'west of 45°W AND cells separated'. Verification is manual comparison against WMM/CHAOS-7 cell-position outputs. No hardcoded value AND no live-fetch; it is a non-monitored predicate.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither. The predicate is evaluated editorially against standard model outputs, not computed inside the dome's pipeline.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The 45°W threshold sits ~35° east of the current western-cell center (~80°W) drifting westward at 0.2–0.3°/yr. At historical drift rates, the cell reversing and crossing 45°W eastward would require >100 years. 'Cells separated' has no minimum separation requirement; any non-zero split satisfies it. Both predicates are trivially satisfied for the indefinite future.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our text makes only the generalized data-reuse claim, which is accurate: these 9 WINs do draw from datasets already exploited in WIN-004..WIN-039.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: W022 is a manual structural-threshold check. No Domain entry for 'SAA cell longitude' exists in the dome's 39+ monitoring domains documented in prior cycles (the dome monitors SAA decay amplitude at Tsumeb station in Domain 8, which does not address cell-centroid longitude). monitor.py remains absent from the raw-text snapshot, but the convergence of prior reviews is conclusive.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: SAA cell longitudes are direct outputs of WMM-2025, CHAOS-7, and IGRF-13 derived from magnetohydrodynamics of reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (Aubert 2015; Terra-Nova et al. 2017). The dome relabels the mechanism as 'aetheric field decay' but adopts the same numerical position. No dome-specific derivation changes the number.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The SAA western cell has been observed west of 45°W with center near 80°W since at least the early 20th century. W022 was registered 2026-03-12, many decades after the observation was well-established. The dome does not register a *future* position; it registers a threshold the cell had already long since cleared.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The dome's field equation H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) is axially (radially) symmetric — it has no azimuthal (longitude) dependence. Longitude-specific features like the American-cell-at-~80°W and African-cell-at-~30°E require azimuthally non-uniform field structure, which no equation on the dome's derivation page provides. The 45°W numeric threshold is adopted, not derived.

holes_found.description: DUPLICATE SENTENCE PERSISTS IN detail_evidence. The sentence 'Notably, the dome's own predictions tracker listed W022 (the prediction corresponding to WIN-040) as PENDING in our raw-text snapshot (2026-03-12), meaning the dome itself had not confirmed this WIN at baseline.' appears VERBATIM TWICE consecutively. This was flagged as moderate in c2 (2026-04-07) and remains unfixed 15 days later. It is visible to every reader who expands the detail block and makes our review look unpolished. Escalating from moderate → major: readers who encounter an obvious copy-paste bug in a detail block lose confidence in the rigor of the adjacent arguments.
holes_found.recommendation: Delete one of the two duplicate sentences in detail_evidence. Exact string to remove (one occurrence): 'Notably, the dome's own predictions tracker listed W022 (the prediction corresponding to WIN-040) as PENDING in our raw-text snapshot (2026-03-12), meaning the dome itself had not confirmed this WIN at baseline. ' Trivial sed fix — has been pending for two weeks.

holes_found.description: TLDR factual error: tldr_evidence describes the grouped batch as '(040-048 batch)' but the actual detail_group spans 040–043, 057b, 059–061, 063 — gapped, ending at 063, not 048. Readers who only read the collapsed TLDR (which is the design intent of progressive disclosure) see a wrong range description. TLDR factual errors are major per the curmudgeon rubric (readers see TLDRs first).
holes_found.recommendation: Replace '(040-048 batch)' with '(the 040–063 magnetic-field batch)' or simply drop the parenthetical. Current detail_group string 'WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063' is also missing 057b; consider updating to 'WIN-040–043, 057b, 059–061, 063'.

holes_found.description: The 45°W threshold claim itself is never engaged with in detail_evidence. The text debunks WIN-040 only through the generic meta-argument 'these are data-reuse duplicates.' A dome defender can correctly respond: 'You dismissed the SAA longitude prediction without even stating what the prediction is or why it is trivial.' The c2 review flagged this exact gap and recommended adding WIN-040-specific text about the ~80°W cell center, 0.2–0.3°/yr drift, and the >100-year falsification horizon. That recommendation was not implemented.
holes_found.recommendation: Add 1–2 sentences (either to the grouped block with a WIN-040 callout, or to detail_extra): 'The SAA western cell centre is at ~80°W drifting westward at 0.2–0.3°/yr (NOAA WMM 2025; Pavón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016). Setting the threshold 35° east of the cell centre makes W022 trivially satisfied for >100 years — it is a description of current geography, not a falsifiable prediction.' Pairs naturally with the azimuthal-symmetry hole below.

holes_found.description: Missing structural argument: the dome's field equation H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) is radially/axially symmetric and CANNOT produce longitude-specific predictions from first principles. This is a strictly stronger argument than 'non-discriminating', because it shows the dome model is structurally incapable of deriving the 45°W value — any longitude predicate must be imported from the globe literature. This argument is absent from the current text but present in c2 review recommendations.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to detail_evidence or detail_extra: 'The dome's field equation H(r) depends only on radial distance r, not on azimuthal angle. Any longitude-specific SAA feature (American cell ~80°W, African cell ~30°E) must be adopted from WMM/CHAOS-7 output — the dome geometry cannot derive it.' This converts the objection from statistical ('non-discriminating') to structural ('incapable of the prediction class'), which is harder to rebut.

holes_found.description: Post-hoc/timestamp boilerplate in detail_verdict_text lists irrelevant datasets. The sentence 'The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)' includes ocean-tide gauges and telluric records, which are not the confirming source for an SAA cell-longitude claim (the confirming source is WMM/CHAOS-7 magnetic-field models). The boilerplate reads as a template pasted without localization. A dome defender could screenshot this exact sentence as evidence that we applied a formula without engaging with the specific WIN.
holes_found.recommendation: Localize the parenthetical to the actual confirming dataset for this WIN, e.g.: 'The confirming value for WIN-040 comes from WMM-2025 / CHAOS-7 SAA cell-position outputs, publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit...' Do the analogous localization for sibling WINs in this group — this is a pattern fix, not a one-off.

holes_found.description: WIN-061 (Schumann suppression during G3 geomagnetic storms) is included in this grouped evidence block but is topically unrelated to SAA longitude / geomagnetic-field-decay. WIN-061 concerns ionospheric physics and Schumann resonance suppression. Its inclusion was flagged in c2 and remains; the jump from SAA cell geography to Schumann resonance inside one 'these 9 WINs' paragraph lets a defender argue we padded the 'duplicate' count. Group by data source (INTERMAGNET/NOAA/Tomsk) is technically defensible but topic coherence is low.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) split WIN-061 into a small Schumann-adjacent subgroup, or (b) add one sentence acknowledging the topical breadth: 'This group spans multiple domains (geomagnetic morphology, pole drift, Schumann resonance) but shares the structural pattern of adopting public dataset values without deriving them from dome geometry.'

holes_found.description: Cross-reference to 'Part 3.1b' (post-hoc carve-out) is live and correctly resolves in docs/index.html (id='posthoc-standard' exists). However, the anchor text reads 'Part 3.1b' — a sub-section of Part 3 under the new V6 numbering. This is correct but potentially confusing since the previous cross-reference in this verdict block was written during the V6 restructure and could have been stale. Flagged only to confirm no stale cross-reference remains.
holes_found.recommendation: No change required. Recording that the cross-reference was audited and resolves.

stronger_arguments.description: The >100-year-falsifiability-horizon argument: cell centre at ~80°W, westward drift 0.2–0.3°/yr, threshold at 45°W ⇒ cell would need to reverse direction and travel 35° east to falsify — no historical precedent for such a reversal on century timescales. This reframes WIN-040 from 'prediction' to 'description of geography with a ~century safety margin'. Stronger than our current generic 'non-discriminating' claim because it quantifies the unfalsifiability.
sources: Pavón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016, Earth-Science Reviews 154:96–125 (historical SAA drift rates)

stronger_arguments.description: Axial-symmetry structural impossibility: H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) has zero azimuthal derivative. The dome cannot produce any SAA longitude prediction from its own equations; it can only adopt WMM/CHAOS outputs. This is a categorical argument (the model is structurally incapable) rather than a quantitative one (the model happens to match).
sources: Dome model derivation page (H(r) equation in 05-model.txt)
Terra-Nova et al. 2017, PNAS 114:3543–3548 (longitude-dependent SAA structure from core-mantle boundary flux patches)

kernel_of_truth.description: The SAA's two-cell structure and the approximate western-cell longitude are real, non-trivial geomagnetic features that took decades of measurement to characterize. The dome author has engaged with current geomagnetic data (WMM/CHAOS) rather than fabricating phenomena. Recognising the American-vs-African cell split and the western cell's approximate longitude is more than most pseudoscience models attempt.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognising the observation is not equivalent to predicting it. (1) The 45°W threshold is ~35° east of the cell centre, making it automatically satisfied for >100 years. (2) The dome's radially symmetric field equation cannot derive longitude-specific features — the number is adopted, not computed. (3) Standard MHD of reversed-flux patches at the core–mantle boundary already predicts SAA cell positions; the dome adds no physical content beyond a renaming of the cause.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender could argue: 'Your critique conflates data-source with derivation. Of course we use WMM — WMM publishes the data; everyone uses WMM. The dome model makes a structural claim: aetheric field decay in an ovoid cavity is consistent with the observed two-cell SAA. Your own standard-MHD models also don't derive specific longitudes; they evolve from arbitrary initial conditions that happen to produce the current pattern. We both fit the data post-hoc. You accuse us of non-discrimination while your preferred model is equally non-discriminating — neither of us pre-registered 45°W in 1900. The 100-year safety margin you cite is therefore a feature of the phenomenon, not a bug in our claim.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add a one-sentence distinction inside the evidence block: 'Standard MHD doesn't pre-register a specific SAA longitude either, but it does derive the two-cell morphology and westward drift rate from first-principles core dynamics; the dome adopts the end state without a derivation path.' This preempts the 'you also post-hoc' comeback by making the discriminator 'derivation path exists vs. does not', not 'prediction was pre-registered'.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-037 claim reads 'Field decay >=28 nT' and WIN-042 reads 'Field decay >=28 nT/year'; our WIN-040 evidence correctly notes they are mathematically equivalent (one-year cumulative vs rate), consistent with WIN-042's own finding text. WIN-043 claim '2.26× longitudinal' is correctly characterized as arithmetic from NOAA trajectory data. WIN-061 (Schumann) is topically distinct but grouped on data-reuse grounds; not an inconsistency per se, just a coherence concern flagged under holes_found. No cross-WIN contradictions in the arguments themselves.

claims_checked: Detail_group WIN count: 'These 9 WINs' vs actual roster in data/wins.json
tldr_evidence range description: '040-048 batch' vs actual batch span
quantitative_verification.computations: Queried data/wins.json for all entries with detail_group === 'WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063' — result: 9 entries (040, 041, 042, 043, 057b, 059, 060, 061, 063). Confirms the '9 WINs' claim in detail_evidence. Verified detail_group label string is missing '057b' (cosmetic). Confirmed tldr_evidence's '040-048 batch' description does not match the actual roster (which extends to 063).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: (1) tldr_evidence label '(040-048 batch)' is factually wrong — flagged as major under holes_found. (2) detail_group string 'WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063' is missing 057b — cosmetic; flagged as minor part of the same recommendation. (3) The '9 WINs' count in detail_evidence is correct.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 change-driven review of WIN-040. c2 was 2026-04-07; detail_verdict_text has since grown from 93 → 1079 chars (91.4%), driven by insertion of a generic post-hoc/timestamp-test paragraph. The verdict string and other field lengths are stable.

THREE PRIOR c2 FINDINGS REMAIN UNFIXED:
  • Duplicate sentence (now escalated major — visible to every reader, 15 days stale)
  • Missing WIN-040-specific engagement with the 45°W threshold
  • Missing axial-symmetry structural argument

TWO NEW FINDINGS IN THIS CYCLE:
  • TLDR range error '040-048 batch' (actual span ends at 063, skips to 057b; readers see TLDRs first — major)
  • Post-hoc boilerplate lists irrelevant datasets (ocean-tide gauges, telluric records) for an SAA longitude claim — reads as un-localized template copy (moderate)

VERDICT HOLDS: 'Std Model Explains' remains correct and no verdict change is warranted. The primary defect is non-discrimination + structural incapacity, not deception, so 'Misleading' would be an over-claim. Escalating to verdict_change would be disproportionate.

ADVOCATE-MODE TENSION: The dome defender's strongest comeback ('you also post-hoc — standard MHD doesn't pre-register specific longitudes either') scores 3/5. It can be defused in one sentence by foregrounding 'derivation path exists vs. does not' as the discriminator, and that single sentence also strengthens the primary argument against non-discrimination.

PRIORITY FOR DECIDER: (1) delete duplicate sentence (trivial, overdue); (2) fix TLDR range string; (3) localize post-hoc boilerplate dataset list; (4) add azimuthal-symmetry sentence + >100-year-horizon quantification; (5) WIN-061 grouping reconsidered or annotated. Items 1–3 are surgical patches; items 4–5 are small expansions.

citations_verified: Anchor #posthoc-standard resolves in docs/index.html (Part 3.1b, id confirmed present once).
